8.8. JUVENILE JUSTICE SYSTEM IN INDIA
Why in the News?
The recently released report “Juvenile Justice and Children in Conflict with the Law: A Study of Capacity at the Frontlines” by the India Justice Report.
More on the News
• The report evaluated the functioning of the Indian Juvenile Justice System ten years after enactment of Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015 (JJ Act, 2015).
• Led by Tata Trusts, in collaboration with civil society organizations, The India Justice Report provides data-driven insights to track improvements and deficits in the formal justice system.
System of Juvenile Justice in India
• Legal Basis: Governed by the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015, which replaced the JJ Act, 2000; amended by the JJ (Care and Protection of Children) Amendment Act, 2021.
• Objective: Consolidates laws relating to children in conflict with law and children in need of care and protection, focusing on care, rehabilitation, and social reintegration.
• Definitions:
o Child: Person who has not completed 18 years of age.
o Juvenile: A child below 18 years.
o Child in conflict with law: A child below 18 years alleged or found to have committed an offence.
• Key Provisions of JJ Act, 2015
